title: Pasteurisation
abstract: An apparatus (1) for processing foodstuffs, the apparatus (1) comprising belt conveying means (2) on which one or more sealed and flexible packages of fluid foodstuff (10, Figure 2) are conveyable, a microwave zone (3) through which said belt conveying means (2) extends to convey said one or more sealed and flexible packages (10), and in which said packages (10) being conveyed through the zone (3) by and upon said belt conveying means (2) are exposed to microwaves, and turbulence inducing means (21, Figure 3) capable of inducing turbulence, preferably periodically and repeatedly inducing turbulence, within said packages (10) supported on said belt conveying means (2) before, whilst in or being conveyed through and/or after the microwave zone (3).
claims: Claims 1. An apparatus for processing foodstuffs, the apparatus comprising belt conveying means on which one or more sealed and flexible packages of fluid foodstuff are conveyable, a microwave zone through which said belt conveying means extends to convey said one or more sealed and flexible packages, and in which said packages being conveyed through the zone by and upon said belt conveying means are exposed to microwaves, and turbulence inducing means capable of inducing turbulence, preferably periodically and repeatedly inducing turbulence, within said packages supported on said belt conveying means before, whilst in or being conveyed through and/or after the microwave zone. 2. Apparatus according to Claim 1 , wherein said turbulence inducing means is capable of periodically and repeatedly inducing turbulence, within said packages supported on said belt conveying means before, whilst in or being conveyed through and/or after the microwave zone. 3. Apparatus according to Claim 1 or 2, wherein said belt conveying means comprises an endless belt conveyor and wherein the endless belt conveyor has, or is arranged to adopt, a concave upper surface for supporting said one or more flexible packages thereupon. 4. Apparatus according to any of Claims 1 , 2 or 3, wherein said belt conveying means is operable to convey plural sealed and flexible packages, each containing foodstuffs of excess of 1 kg and preferably in excess of 2kg, for example in excess of 3kg. 5. Apparatus according to any of any preceding Claim, wherein said belt conveying means comprises plural conveyors to facilitate cleaning of the conveying means in sections. 6. Apparatus according to any preceding Claim, wherein the microwave zone comprises by a microwave tunnel comprising plural magnetrons. 7. Apparatus according to any preceding Claim, further comprising heat detection means to determine a temperature or heat profile within a package being conveyed. 8. Apparatus according to any preceding Claim, wherein said turbulence inducing means comprises means to physically induce turbulence within a conveyed sealed and flexible package. 9. Apparatus according to any preceding Claim, wherein said turbulence inducing means comprises one or more bodies over which said belt conveying means travels to describe an undulating path, thereby to cause turbulence with a package conveyed by said belt conveying means. 10. Apparatus according to any preceding Claim, wherein said turbulence inducing means comprises abutment means arranged to impart a force on, for example by contact, a package as it is conveyed. 1 1. Apparatus according to Claim 10, wherein said abutment means periodically contacts the package. 12. Apparatus according to Claims 10 or 11 , wherein said abutment means comprises one or more reciprocating or reciprocable members arranged to intermittently or periodically contact the package as it is conveyed. 13. Apparatus according to Claims 10 or 1 1 , wherein said abutment means is operable to periodically force a fluid, for example air, against a package as it is conveyed relative to said abutment means. 14. Apparatus according to any preceding Claim, further comprising package filling apparatus, arranged to fill a package with fluid foodstuff upstream of the conveying means. 15. Apparatus according to Claim 14, further comprising a transfer conveyor to convey filled packages from the package filling apparatus to said conveying means. 16. Apparatus according to any preceding Claim, further comprising chilling means downstream of the microwave zone and arranged to cool packages. 17. A method of pasteurising a fluid foodstuff contained in a sealed package, the method comprising providing a fluid foodstuff in a sealed package and conveying the package on a belt conveyor through a microwave zone and inducing turbulence within the package whilst it is supported on the belt conveyor to mix the contents thereof. 18. A method of pasteurising packages of sauces, wherein the packages are flexible and have a mass of over 2kg, the method comprising exposing the packages to microwaves to pasteurise the sauce and thereby to improve the shelf life of the sauce. 19. A sealed package of sauce having a total mass of over 2kg and a shelf life of over fifteen days. 20. A sealed package of sauce according to Claim 19, wherein the sauce is fabricated from fresh ingredients and has been pasteurised by exposure to microwaves. 21. A sealed package of fluid foodstuff formed using the apparatus of any of Claims 1 to 16 or the method of Claim 17 or 18.
